datasets.name: mme_service_requests
datasets.description: UE-initiated service request procedures with EMM cause codes, latency, and authentication outcomes. Multiple events per MME host per hour to support anomaly detection.
datasets.cardinality_requirements: 2-5 mme_host values share the same (cluster_id, datacenter) combination for split-brain detection. 20-30 unique imsi_hash per tracking_area_code per hour for authentication failure analysis.

datasets.name: mme_hosts
datasets.description: MME/AMF host configuration and capacity information for enriching service request events

datasets.name: hss_authentication_events
datasets.description: HSS database authentication requests with query performance, replication lag, and Diameter result codes. High volume to support cardinality analysis of affected subscribers.

datasets.name: hss_database_nodes
datasets.description: HSS database cluster node configuration and capacity metrics

datasets.name: cell_handoff_events
datasets.description: Cell tower handoff attempts and outcomes with geographic coordinates and failure causes. Multiple handoffs per cell pair per hour.

datasets.name: cell_sites
datasets.description: Cell tower configuration with geographic coordinates and operational state

datasets.name: signaling_security_alerts
datasets.description: SS7/Diameter security alerts from signaling firewall with attack patterns and roaming partner information

datasets.name: roaming_partners
datasets.description: Roaming partner network information and risk scoring

datasets.name: tracking_areas
datasets.description: Tracking area configuration and geographic mapping

queries.name: Mass Authentication Failure Detection with IMSI Cardinality Analysis
queries.pain_point: Reactive incident response - NOC learns about HSS database corruption through customer complaints after 20-30 help desk calls, with MTTD exceeding 15-20 minutes
queries.description: Detect HSS database corruption or replication lag within 60 seconds by monitoring sudden drops in authentication success rates and calculating affected subscriber cardinality per tracking area. Alerts when 50+ unique IMSI hashes experience failures in 5-minute windows, distinguishing database issues from legitimate failures.
queries.esql_query: FROM hss_authentication_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 30 minutes | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS failed_auth_count = COUNT(*) WHERE auth_outcome == "failure", unique_failed_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi_hash) WHERE auth_outcome == "failure", avg_replication_lag = AVG(replication_lag_seconds), p95_query_duration = PERCENTILE(auth_query_duration_ms, 95) BY time_bucket, hss_host, tracking_area_code | LOOKUP JOIN hss_database_nodes ON hss_host | LOOKUP JOIN tracking_areas ON tracking_area_code | INLINESTATS baseline_failed_imsi = AVG(unique_failed_imsi), stddev_failed_imsi = STD_DEV(unique_failed_imsi) BY tracking_area_code | EVAL z_score_imsi = (unique_failed_imsi - baseline_failed_imsi) / COALESCE(stddev_failed_imsi, 1) | WHERE unique_failed_imsi >= 50 OR z_score_imsi > 3 OR avg_replication_lag > 5 | EVAL incident_severity = CASE(unique_failed_imsi > 200 AND avg_replication_lag > 10, "CRITICAL", unique_failed_imsi > 100 OR avg_replication_lag > 5, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, hss_host, tracking_areas.area_name, unique_failed_imsi, incident_severity, avg_replication_lag, hss_database_nodes.node_role, hss_database_nodes.cluster_name, z_score_imsi, failed_auth_count | SORT unique_failed_imsi DESC

queries.name: MME Split-Brain Detection via Duplicate GUTI Assignment Analysis
queries.pain_point: Core network split-brain scenarios cause signaling storms generating 50K-100K messages/sec, overwhelming HSS capacity and causing network-wide authentication failures
queries.description: Detect MME cluster split-brain scenarios by identifying when multiple MME hosts within the same cluster process service requests for identical IMSI hashes simultaneously, indicating duplicate active sessions. Alerts 3-5 minutes before signaling storms reach critical thresholds.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_service_requests | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 15 minutes | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS unique_mme_hosts = COUNT_DISTINCT(mme_host), total_requests = COUNT(*), failed_requests = COUNT(*) WHERE service_request_outcome == "failure" BY time_bucket, cluster_id, datacenter, imsi_hash | WHERE unique_mme_hosts >= 2 | STATS duplicate_imsi_count = COUNT(*), affected_clusters = COUNT_DISTINCT(cluster_id), max_mme_hosts = MAX(unique_mme_hosts), total_failed = SUM(failed_requests) BY time_bucket, cluster_id, datacenter | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON cluster_id | WHERE duplicate_imsi_count >= 10 | EVAL split_brain_severity = CASE(duplicate_imsi_count > 100, "CRITICAL_SIGNALING_STORM", duplicate_imsi_count > 50, "HIGH_SPLIT_BRAIN", "MEDIUM_ANOMALY") | KEEP time_bucket, cluster_id, datacenter, duplicate_imsi_count, split_brain_severity, max_mme_hosts, mme_hosts.pool_name, total_failed, mme_hosts.software_version | SORT duplicate_imsi_count DESC

queries.name: Cell Tower Handoff Cascade Failure with Geographic Correlation
queries.pain_point: Radio equipment failures create coverage black holes affecting 8-10 neighboring cells, but NOC lacks ability to detect cascading handoff failures and geographic impact patterns
queries.description: Identify single cell tower failures causing cascading handoff failures to multiple neighboring cells by analyzing cell-pair handoff success rates and detecting when one source cell shows degraded handoffs to 5+ target cells. Provides geographic coordinates for field dispatch.
queries.esql_query: FROM cell_handoff_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 1 hours | EVAL hour_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS handoff_attempts = COUNT(*), handoff_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE handoff_outcome == "failure", unique_target_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(target_cell_id) BY hour_bucket, source_cell_id, geographic_region | EVAL handoff_success_rate_pct = ((handoff_attempts - handoff_failures) / handoff_attempts) * 100 | INLINESTATS baseline_success_rate = AVG(handoff_success_rate_pct), p5_success_rate = PERCENTILE(handoff_success_rate_pct, 5) BY geographic_region | WHERE handoff_success_rate_pct < 85 AND unique_target_cells >= 5 | LOOKUP JOIN cell_sites ON source_cell_id=cell_id | EVAL coverage_impact = CASE(handoff_success_rate_pct < 50 AND unique_target_cells >= 8, "CRITICAL_BLACKHOLE", handoff_success_rate_pct < 70, "HIGH_COVERAGE_DEGRADATION", "MEDIUM_HANDOFF_ISSUES") | KEEP source_cell_id, cell_sites.cell_name, handoff_success_rate_pct, unique_target_cells, coverage_impact, cell_sites.latitude, cell_sites.longitude, cell_sites.vendor, cell_sites.operational_state, handoff_failures | SORT handoff_success_rate_pct ASC

queries.name: SS7 Location Tracking Attack Detection with Roaming Partner Risk Analysis
queries.pain_point: SS7 signaling networks vulnerable to location tracking attacks via compromised roaming partners, with attacks persisting for days before manual log analysis detects them
queries.description: Detect SS7 location tracking and SMS interception attacks within 30-60 seconds by correlating signaling firewall alerts with roaming partner baseline traffic patterns. Uses semantic search to identify attack descriptions and calculates partner risk scores based on historical attack patterns.
queries.esql_query: FROM signaling_security_alerts | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 2 hours AND (alert_type == "location_tracking" OR alert_type == "sms_interception" OR SEMANTIC(alert_description, "location tracking SMS interception IMSI probing attacks")) | EVAL hour_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS total_attacks = COUNT(*), blocked_attacks = COUNT(*) WHERE firewall_action == "blocked", total_affected_imsi = SUM(affected_imsi_count), avg_message_rate = AVG(message_rate) BY hour_bucket, source_plmn, alert_type | LOOKUP JOIN roaming_partners ON source_plmn=partner_plmn | INLINESTATS baseline_attacks = AVG(total_attacks) BY alert_type | EVAL message_rate_deviation_pct = ((avg_message_rate - roaming_partners.baseline_message_rate) / roaming_partners.baseline_message_rate) * 100 | WHERE total_attacks > baseline_attacks * 2 OR message_rate_deviation_pct > 300 OR total_affected_imsi >= 20 | EVAL attack_severity = CASE(total_affected_imsi > 50 AND blocked_attacks < total_attacks * 0.5, "CRITICAL_ACTIVE_BREACH", total_affected_imsi > 20, "HIGH_ATTACK_VOLUME", "MEDIUM_SUSPICIOUS_ACTIVITY") | KEEP source_plmn, roaming_partners.partner_name, alert_type, total_attacks, total_affected_imsi, attack_severity, roaming_partners.risk_score, message_rate_deviation_pct, roaming_partners.partner_country, blocked_attacks | SORT total_affected_imsi DESC

queries.name: Proactive MME Service Request Failure Root Cause Analysis with Procedure Stage Breakdown
queries.pain_point: Service request failures troubleshooting takes 2-3 hours of multi-team manual log correlation across radio, core network, and database tiers to identify failure stage
queries.description: Analyze complete service request procedure flow to identify failure stage (S1-AP, MME processing, S6a authentication, S11 bearer creation) and pinpoint root causes. Calculates procedure stage latency percentiles and correlates failures with EMM cause codes to distinguish radio, core network, and database issues.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_service_requests | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 4 hours | EVAL hour_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS total_requests = COUNT(*), s1ap_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE s1ap_result == "failure", auth_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE s6a_auth_duration_ms > 3000 OR service_request_outcome == "failure" AND emm_cause_code IN (17, 18, 19), bearer_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE s11_bearer_result == "failure", p95_procedure_duration = PERCENTILE(procedure_duration_ms, 95), p95_auth_duration = PERCENTILE(s6a_auth_duration_ms, 95) BY hour_bucket, mme_host, tracking_area_code | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN tracking_areas ON tracking_area_code | EVAL total_failures = s1ap_failures + auth_failures + bearer_failures | EVAL failure_rate_pct = (total_failures / total_requests) * 100 | INLINESTATS baseline_failure_rate = AVG(failure_rate_pct), p90_failure_rate = PERCENTILE(failure_rate_pct, 90) BY tracking_area_code | WHERE failure_rate_pct > baseline_failure_rate * 3 OR failure_rate_pct > 5 | EVAL primary_failure_stage = CASE(s1ap_failures > auth_failures AND s1ap_failures > bearer_failures, "RADIO_S1AP", auth_failures > bearer_failures, "HSS_AUTHENTICATION", "SGW_BEARER_ESTABLISHMENT") | EVAL root_cause_severity = CASE(failure_rate_pct > 10, "CRITICAL", failure_rate_pct > 5, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP hour_bucket, mme_host, tracking_areas.area_name, failure_rate_pct, primary_failure_stage, root_cause_severity, s1ap_failures, auth_failures, bearer_failures, mme_hosts.vendor, mme_hosts.software_version | SORT failure_rate_pct DESC

queries.name: Subscriber Tier Impact Analysis for SLA Violation Prediction
queries.pain_point: NOC lacks real-time visibility into subscriber impact metrics needed to make rapid mitigation decisions, with each minute of degradation triggering $50K-$150K in SLA penalties
queries.description: Calculate real-time affected subscriber counts by tier (VIP, Premium, Free) and geographic distribution to predict SLA violation thresholds. Alerts when impacted subscriber cardinality exceeds 0.1% of regional base, enabling proactive traffic steering and mitigation decisions.
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_service_requests | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 15 minutes AND service_request_outcome == "failure" | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS affected_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi_hash), total_failures = COUNT(*) BY time_bucket, subscriber_tier, mme_host, tracking_area_code | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN tracking_areas ON tracking_area_code | STATS total_affected_subscribers = SUM(affected_subscribers), total_failure_events = SUM(total_failures), affected_tracking_areas = COUNT_DISTINCT(tracking_area_code) BY time_bucket, subscriber_tier, mme_hosts.deployment_region | EVAL sla_breach_risk = CASE(subscriber_tier == "VIP" AND total_affected_subscribers > 50, "CRITICAL_SLA_BREACH", subscriber_tier == "Premium" AND total_affected_subscribers > 200, "HIGH_SLA_RISK", total_affected_subscribers > 500, "MEDIUM_SLA_RISK", "LOW_RISK") | WHERE total_affected_subscribers >= 100 OR sla_breach_risk IN ("CRITICAL_SLA_BREACH", "HIGH_SLA_RISK") | KEEP time_bucket, subscriber_tier, mme_hosts.deployment_region, total_affected_subscribers, sla_breach_risk, affected_tracking_areas, total_failure_events | SORT total_affected_subscribers DESC

queries.name: Semantic Search for Similar Network Incident Patterns
queries.pain_point: Engineers manually search through historical incident logs across 5-7 vendor consoles to find similar past issues, extending MTTR by 30-45 minutes per incident
queries.description: Use semantic search to find similar historical security incidents based on natural language descriptions of attack patterns, enabling rapid identification of known attack vectors and proven mitigation strategies from past incidents.
queries.esql_query: FROM signaling_security_alerts | WHERE SEMANTIC(alert_description, "?incident_description") | LOOKUP JOIN roaming_partners ON source_plmn=partner_plmn | STATS incident_count = COUNT(*), total_affected = SUM(affected_imsi_count), avg_severity_score = AVG(CASE(alert_severity == "critical", 4, alert_severity == "high", 3, alert_severity == "medium", 2, 1)) BY alert_type, roaming_partners.partner_name, roaming_partners.risk_score | WHERE incident_count >= 2 | EVAL historical_pattern_risk = CASE(avg_severity_score > 3.5 AND total_affected > 100, "HIGH_RECURRENCE_RISK", avg_severity_score > 2.5, "MEDIUM_RECURRENCE_RISK", "LOW_RECURRENCE_RISK") | KEEP alert_type, roaming_partners.partner_name, incident_count, total_affected, historical_pattern_risk, roaming_partners.risk_score, avg_severity_score | SORT incident_count DESC | LIMIT 10

parameters.name: incident_description
parameters.description: Natural language description of the current incident pattern to find similar historical cases
